allies.rationale_text: Intellectual-humility makes growth-mindset real by turning weakness recognition into the starting point for improvement instead of a threat to ego.
allies.source_quote: The growth perspective requires intellectual humility-acknowledging what you don't know.

allies.rationale_text: Deliberate-practice gives growth-mindset an operational path by converting the belief that capability can expand into feedback-linked reps and measurable improvement.
allies.source_quote: This deliberate practice helps the new mindset become more automatic over time.

allies.rationale_text: Resilience supports growth-mindset by helping people treat discomfort and setbacks as part of development instead of as evidence they should stop.
allies.source_quote: It provides the mental toughness and resilience necessary to view difficulties as a challenge.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias blocks growth-mindset when people protect the current self-story instead of honestly updating from disconfirming evidence or criticism.
antagonists.source_quote: These are model-defending behaviors where individuals seek information that confirms existing beliefs.

antagonists.rationale_text: Constraints check growth-mindset by preventing improvement language from hand-waving away structural limits, missing fit, or resource reality.
antagonists.source_quote: Danger When Growth Language Denies Real Constraints.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Growth-mindset conflicts with base-rates when belief in growth potential overrides statistical evidence about the probability of improvement given prior performance data.
structured_tensions.source_quote: growth-mindset conflicts with base-rates (base rates) when belief in growth potential overrides statistical evidence about the probability of improvement given prior performance data.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Growth-mindset conflicts with sunk-cost-fallacy when growth-mindset persistence at a failing endeavor becomes indistinguishable from escalation of commitment.
structured_tensions.source_quote: growth-mindset conflicts with sunk-cost-fallacy (sunk cost fallacy) when growth-mindset persistence at a failing endeavor becomes indistinguishable from escalation of commitment.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on feedback-friendly adaptation, humility, and deliberate improvement, with one important guardrail around real constraints.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader culture-transformation relation sprawl because the cleanest current-vocabulary links are humility, deliberate-practice, resilience, and confirmation-bias.
open_questions: The constraints antagonist is a reviewed normalization of the explicit anti-hand-waving warning rather than a named explicit pair block.
